text.title: Model Upgrade Migration Playbook Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Vendor outage on new model. No kill-switch. Production down for hours.
antipattern.root-cause: Migration shipped without named rollback plan or flag.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory rollback plan with flag, baseline id, step-down sequence before promotion.

antipattern.symptom: All 12 prompts promoted at once. 3 regress. Triage takes days while users hit broken paths.
antipattern.root-cause: Per-prompt eval skipped; fleet promoted as bundle.
antipattern.fix: Per-prompt eval + triage gate; only prompts within contract promote, others queue for prompt rework.

antipattern.symptom: Migration record reads 'we switched to 4.7 because it's better'. Future operator cannot replay decision.
antipattern.root-cause: Decision lacks input citations (vendor URL, eval data, cost analysis).
antipattern.fix: Every decision cites the input artefact(s) that justified it; un-citable decisions blocked at review.

antipattern.symptom: Cost estimate based on outdated price card. Actual bill 2x projected.
antipattern.root-cause: Cost dimension not pulled from live pricing endpoint at migration time.
antipattern.fix: Pull pricing at migration-start; record snapshot; re-validate at promotion.
